meaning: adj.复杂的,错综的,难以理解的
examples: He knows his way around the intricate maze of patent law.
His new play, Arcadia, is as intricate, elaborate and allusive as anything he has yet written.
You develop in-depth knowledge of the intricate structure of the language.
He showed me into a lovely rose-coloured room with intricate plasterwork on the ceiling and walls.
Turkish artists hand-painted the walls with intricate Ottoman designs and the bathrooms have hammam-style basins.
